description: Baseline: Focuster workload benchmark (100 events chop + 100 action schedule loop)

asi.hypothesis: baseline measurement
asi.workload: 100 busy events chop + 10 non-work-hour chops + 100x (mergeOverlaps + searchByLengthStartingAt + chop)

description: Baseline: scaled Focuster workload (1000 events, 1500 actions, 100 work days)

asi.hypothesis: baseline measurement with scaled workload

description: Replace spread+map in updateAttributes() with simple for-loops — 5.2x faster

asi.hypothesis: Math.min/max with spread+map allocates arrays on every call in the hottest loop
asi.key_insight: updateAttributes called on every insert/remove/rotate, spread+map was creating temp arrays thousands of times per operation

description: Optimize toArray() — single result array instead of concat chains — 18% faster

asi.hypothesis: toArray creates many intermediate arrays via concat, pass result array through recursion instead

description: Add dirty flag to skip redundant mergeOverlaps() — 3.2x faster (182→58ms)

asi.hypothesis: mergeOverlaps rebuilds tree from scratch every call; dirty flag skips when tree unchanged since last merge
asi.key_insight: In schedule loop, mergeOverlaps called 1500x but tree only modified by chop each iteration, so each merge is immediately followed by a chop then another merge — dirty flag eliminates 99.9% of rebuilds
asi.checks_fix: constructor must set _dirty=true so initial mergeOverlaps works

description: O(n) balanced bulk build for fromIntervals() instead of O(n log n) sequential inserts — 1.8x faster

asi.hypothesis: fromIntervals does n sorted inserts (O(n log n)); recursive midpoint split builds balanced tree in O(n)
asi.key_insight: needed to handle same-start grouping and dedup in 2-element base case

description: Simplify chop() — single searchOverlap instead of 2x searchPoint + removeEnveloped

asi.hypothesis: chop did 2x searchPoint + searchOverlap (via removeEnveloped) + filter; replaced with single searchOverlap + classify

description: Avoid [boolean] array allocation in insert() — use shared module-level scratch arrays

asi.hypothesis: insert allocates 2 [boolean] arrays per top-level call; use module-level shared arrays with comma-operator reset in defaults

description: Remove assert from Interval constructor — no meaningful improvement

asi.hypothesis: assert() in Interval constructor adds overhead
asi.result: no measurable difference — assert cost is negligible compared to tree operations
asi.next_action_hint: focus on schedule loop — mergeOverlaps sort or searchByLengthStartingAt

description: Convert search methods to for-loops + direct #branch access, inline length calc

asi.hypothesis: forEach closures and optional chaining on branch() add overhead in hot search paths
asi.changes: searchPoint, searchOverlap, searchByLengthStartingAt all converted to for-loops with direct #branch[LEFT/RIGHT] access

description: In-order toArray() + eliminate redundant toSorted() calls — 14% faster

asi.hypothesis: toArray with in-order traversal returns sorted results naturally, making toSorted redundant
asi.key_insight: mergeOverlaps, chopAll, toSorted all called toArray().toSorted() — now just toArray()

description: Eliminate sort in searchByLengthStartingAt — in-order traversal already sorted — 34% faster

asi.hypothesis: searchByLengthStartingAt sorted its results but tree traversal already yields sorted order

description: Fix: restore sort in searchByLengthStartingAt — previous 15.31ms was incorrect (unsorted results). Correct baseline is ~22ms.

asi.hypothesis: correctness fix — previous experiment removed sort but traversal wasn't in-order, producing unsorted results
asi.key_insight: searchByLengthStartingAt traverses self→left→right (not in-order) due to shouldSkipBranch pruning, so sort is required

description: Add comprehensive tests + fix bulk build same-start grouping + restore toSorted sort contract

asi.hypothesis: correctness hardening — add 21 new tests covering searchByLengthStartingAt, sort ordering, chop, mergeOverlaps, bulk build
asi.bugs_found: bulk build didn't group same-start intervals from both sides of mid; toSorted was returning unsorted when multiple values share start
asi.fixes: expand group bidirectionally from mid; restore compareIntervals sort in toSorted()

description: Replace toSorted with insertion sort in searchByLengthStartingAt — slower than V8 Timsort

asi.hypothesis: insertion sort on nearly-sorted data should be faster than generic sort
asi.result: V8 Timsort is already optimized for nearly-sorted data and beats manual insertion sort
asi.next_action_hint: try making Node.searchByLengthStartingAt do in-order traversal, or try batch init with chopAll

description: In-order traversal in searchByLengthStartingAt + remove sort — correct this time, tests pass

asi.hypothesis: shouldSkipBranch prunes the whole subtree, so after passing that check, in-order traversal of children yields sorted results without needing a post-sort
asi.key_insight: previous attempt failed because traversal was self→left→right; now left→self→right with subtree-level pruning preserved

description: Use chopAll in benchmark init — 2.8x faster init (models what Focuster should do)

asi.hypothesis: chopAll does single linear sweep instead of 1000 individual chop() calls
asi.note: This is a benchmark change showing chopAll benefit. Focuster should adopt tree.chopAll(busyEvents) instead of events.forEach(e => tree.chop(e.start, e.end))

description: Fast-path chop for single overlap — no improvement

asi.hypothesis: single-overlap fast path avoids array iteration overhead
asi.result: no measurable difference — the overhead is in remove/add tree operations, not the loop
asi.next_action_hint: try optimizing remove() itself or avoid creating Interval objects in chop

description: Micro-opts: direct #branch in balance/updateHeight, avoid slice in mergeOverlaps, ternary max

asi.hypothesis: balance getter used optional chaining on this.left/right instead of direct #branch access; updateHeight used Math.max; mergeOverlaps used slice(1)

description: Optimize remove() — direct #branch access, for-loop findIndex, avoid optional chaining

asi.hypothesis: remove uses this.left/right getters and findIndex closure; direct #branch and for-loop are faster

description: Optimize insert() — direct #branch access, for-loop duplicate check

asi.hypothesis: insert uses branch()/setBranch() method calls and .some() closure; direct array access and for-loop are faster

description: Optimize rotation methods with direct #branch access — no improvement

asi.hypothesis: rotation methods use branch()/setBranch() indirection
asi.result: rotations are infrequent — not a bottleneck
asi.next_action_hint: try reducing Interval object allocation in chop, or profile to find actual bottleneck

description: Eliminate intermediate arrays in chop — no improvement

asi.hypothesis: chop creates toRemove/toAdd arrays; inline remove+add avoids allocation
asi.result: array allocation not the bottleneck, tree operations dominate

description: Add O(1) size counter, first()/last() O(log n) methods — features for Focuster

asi.hypothesis: size was O(n) via toArray().length; first/last required toSorted/toArray. Now O(1) and O(log n) respectively.
asi.implementation: module-level _insertWasDuplicate/_removeSucceeded flags avoid overhead; countIntervals for constructor; min()/max() walk branches

description: Add property-based tests for searchByLengthStartingAt/mergeOverlaps/searchOverlap/size + fix ArrayIntervalCollection bugs + restore sort correctness

asi.hypothesis: property-based tests catch bugs unit tests miss
asi.regression_reason: restored sort in searchByLengthStartingAt since in-order traversal not guaranteed with shouldSkipBranch

description: In-order searchByLengthStartingAt with per-child pruning — eliminates sort, 5.6% faster

asi.hypothesis: previous in-order attempt failed because shouldSkipBranch at parent level could skip left child incorrectly; per-child pruning checks each child individually
asi.key_insight: inline the shouldSkipBranch checks for left and right separately, allowing in-order traversal while preserving pruning correctness

description: In-order searchOverlap with per-child pruning — 4.5% faster

asi.hypothesis: searchOverlap also benefits from in-order traversal with per-child pruning

description: Add findFirstByLengthStartingAt with early termination — O(log n) for first match, 4.1% faster

asi.hypothesis: schedule loop only uses slots[0]; findFirst with early termination skips traversing rest of tree
asi.note: Focuster should use this instead of searchByLengthStartingAt when only first result needed (WholeActionScheduler, SplitActionScheduler)

description: Optimize clone() — slice() instead of spread, direct #branch access

asi.hypothesis: clone uses [...values] spread and this.left/right getters; slice() and direct #branch are faster

description: Optimize _buildBalanced dedup + direct #branch — slight regression

asi.hypothesis: sorted dedup can use adjacent check instead of some(); direct #branch in buildBalanced
asi.result: slight regression, possibly dedup logic issue with backwards expansion

description: chopKnownInterval + findFirstRaw to skip searchOverlap — within noise, adds complexity

asi.hypothesis: schedule loop does findFirst then chop, chop re-searches with searchOverlap; skipping that search should save time
asi.result: within noise — searchOverlap on ~50-node tree is already ~200ns
asi.rollback_reason: adds complexity (2 new methods) for no measurable gain
asi.next_action_hint: try flat values optimization or completely different structural approach

description: Replace assert() with if-throw in IntervalTree.ts — no improvement

asi.hypothesis: assert module import adds overhead
asi.result: no measurable difference — V8 optimizes assert calls well in hot paths

description: Replace #branch private array with _left/_right direct fields — slight schedule improvement

asi.hypothesis: #branch private array has V8 brand check overhead + array indexing; direct fields are faster
asi.note: marginal total improvement, but removes private field overhead and simplifies code

description: Replace Interval #private fields with readonly public — slower (V8 optimizes #private + getters better)

asi.hypothesis: removing getter indirection should be faster
asi.result: V8 optimizes #private fields + getters into inline property access; readonly public fields create a different hidden class shape that's worse
asi.confirmed_dead_end: tried twice now, both times slower

description: In-place replaceInterval in chop — property test caught duplicate bug

asi.hypothesis: replace interval in-place instead of remove+add saves one tree walk
asi.result: 14.28ms (5.3% faster) but fails property test — replaceInterval creates duplicates when another interval with the replacement's end already exists in the same node
asi.rollback_reason: correctness bug: duplicate intervals after replace
asi.next_action_hint: complexity of edge cases not worth ~5% gain; try structural approaches instead

description: Confirmation run — no code change, JIT warmup variance. True baseline is ~13.1-13.5ms (87x faster)

asi.hypothesis: re-measuring to confirm true performance after session restart
asi.note: previous 15.08ms may have included cold-JIT overhead; multiple runs now consistently show 13.1-13.5ms

description: Shared scratch array for remove rebalance flag — within noise

asi.hypothesis: remove allocates [false] on every top-level call; shared scratch avoids allocation
asi.result: within noise — V8 handles small array allocation efficiently

description: Replace splice with swap-pop in remove — regression, V8 deopt likely

asi.hypothesis: splice is O(n), swap-pop is O(1) for removing from values array
asi.result: regression — values.length=0 or the swap may cause V8 hidden class change

description: Inline constructor attributes — within noise

asi.hypothesis: avoid updateAttributes() method call in constructor for single-value case
asi.result: within noise, init slightly better but total unchanged

description: Skip redundant sort in mergeOverlaps/chopAll via fromSortedIntervals — 9% faster

asi.hypothesis: mergeOverlaps and chopAll rebuild tree from already-sorted intervals; fromIntervals unnecessarily re-sorts
asi.key_insight: toSorted on already-sorted data still has O(n) comparison + array copy overhead

description: Inline remove+add in chop, eliminate toRemove/toAdd arrays — slight regression

asi.hypothesis: intermediate arrays in chop add allocation overhead
asi.result: slight regression, V8 optimizes small array allocation well

description: Simplify updateAttributes — skip minStart scan (all values share same start), reduce loop work

asi.hypothesis: all values in a node share the same start (tree invariant), so minStart from values is always this.start — skip scanning

description: Derive maxLength from maxEnd in updateAttributes — slight regression

asi.hypothesis: maxLength = maxEnd - start for same-start values, can skip per-element len computation
asi.result: slight regression, likely V8 JIT prefers the loop with both computations

description: Simplify searchOverlap with early termination on this.start >= end — slight regression

asi.hypothesis: since all values share this.start, can skip overlap check and right subtree when this.start >= end
asi.result: slight regression — the added branch is more expensive than the simplified inner check saves

description: Guard Interval constructor asserts with DEBUG — init better but schedule worse, net regression

asi.hypothesis: Interval constructor's 3 assert calls run on every new Interval even in production
asi.result: init improved 6% but schedule regressed 7% — adding if(DEBUG) branch may change constructor hidden class/JIT optimization
asi.confirmed_dead_end: assert overhead in Interval constructor is not the bottleneck

description: Preserve dirty flag in chop — chop never creates overlaps, eliminates 341 unnecessary mergeOverlaps rebuilds. 2.6x schedule improvement!

asi.hypothesis: chop calls remove+add which set _dirty=true, causing mergeOverlaps to rebuild after every chop. But chop only splits intervals, never creating overlaps.
asi.key_insight: 341 chop hits were each followed by a full mergeOverlaps rebuild (toArray + scan + fromSortedIntervals on ~250 intervals). Preserving dirty state eliminates all of them.

description: Also preserve dirty flag in chopAll — correctness fix, same perf

asi.hypothesis: chopAll also sets dirty unnecessarily
asi.result: no perf change since init dirty was already cleared by first mergeOverlaps in schedule loop, but correct fix
